title: Chraime
summary:
Chraime (Hebrew: חריימה, Arabic: حرايمي haraime) is a spicy fish stew with tomatoes from Northern Africa. The name of the dish comes from the Arabic word for "hot".
Chraime is traditionally eaten by Jews on Erev Shabbat as well as on Rosh Hashanah and Passover for the Seder. Libyan-Jewish immigrants have popularized the dish in Israel.
